seoTitle: Add a Row in a Confluence Table (2026 Quick Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to add a row in a Confluence table using the 2026 hover controls, floating plus icon, or Tab key shortcut without breaking your table layout.
intro: Expanding a table shouldn't mean rebuilding its formatting from scratch. This guide covers the fastest ways to insert new rows into a Confluence table in 2026, whether you prefer clicking, hovering, or a keyboard shortcut.

steps.heading: Open the table in edit mode
steps.body: Enter edit mode on your page and click inside the target table container.

steps.heading: Reveal the row controls
steps.body: Hover your cursor over the outer left border of an existing row to reveal the hidden control strip.

steps.heading: Insert a row with the plus icon
steps.body: Click the floating "+" icon to instantly insert a fresh row directly below that position.

steps.heading: Or use the table menu
steps.body: Click inside a cell and use the top floating table menu to choose Insert Row Below as an alternative method.

steps.heading: Use the Tab shortcut
steps.body: Navigate to the last cell of your bottom row and press Tab to auto-generate a matching new row instantly.

steps.heading: Reorder if needed
steps.body: Grab the left grid selector handle to drag and reposition your new row, then publish to save the update.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to add a row to a Confluence table?
faq.answer: Pressing Tab while in the last cell of the bottom row instantly generates a new matching row without opening any menus.

faq.question: Will adding a row break my existing table formatting?
faq.answer: No, Confluence's editor maintains cell dimensions and inherited formatting rules when a new row is inserted.

faq.question: Can I reorder rows after adding them?
faq.answer: Yes, use the left-hand grid handle to drag and slide rows into a new position.

faq.question: Can I insert a row above an existing one instead of below?
faq.answer: Yes, the floating table menu lets you choose to insert above or below your current position.
